skill.name: ocr
skill.description: Convert PDF and image files into Markdown using Claude vision OCR, with single-file and batch workflows.
skill.summary: Extract text, tables, diagrams, and document structure from PDFs and images into Markdown.
skill.author: Bae-ChangHyun

security_audit.summary: The static external command, weak cryptography, and entropy alerts mostly come from Markdown examples, inline backticks, Korean text, and supported file extensions. No malicious intent, network access, credential theft, or executable payload was found. Medium risk remains because the skill instructs agents to inspect user-supplied paths with shell examples, read untrusted documents, and write Markdown outputs without explicit prompt-injection or overwrite safeguards.

medium_findings.title: Unquoted Shell Path Examples
medium_findings.description: The workflow shows shell commands using user-supplied path placeholders, including path listing and glob expansion. If an agent substitutes a raw path into these commands, shell metacharacters could be interpreted.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are Markdown examples, not executable source code, but the skill instructs agents to inspect user-provided paths. The risk is credible if the examples are followed with raw shell substitution.

medium_findings.title: Untrusted OCR Content Lacks Prompt-Injection Boundary
medium_findings.description: The skill reads PDFs and images and converts their contents without instructing the agent to treat document text as untrusted data. A malicious document could include instructions that try to redirect the OCR workflow.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The workflow clearly processes untrusted document content, but no malicious sample is present in the skill file. The missing boundary is a realistic design weakness for OCR agents.

low_findings.title: Predictable Output Writes May Overwrite Markdown Files
low_findings.description: The workflow writes generated Markdown beside the source file using predictable names. This is expected OCR behavior, but it may overwrite an existing Markdown file if the agent does not check first.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file writes are explicitly required by the workflow and are legitimate for OCR. The concern is limited to missing overwrite checks rather than malicious file modification.

low_findings.title: Static Crypto and Entropy Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography and entropy alerts do not correspond to cryptographic code, encoded payloads, or binary content. The flagged lines are Markdown front matter, Korean text, option descriptions, and OCR examples.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic function, encrypted blob, or executable payload at the cited locations. The static signals are explained by natural-language Markdown content.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Commands With User-Controlled Path Placeholders
dangerous_patterns.description: Examples use shell commands around file paths supplied by the user. Agents should prefer structured file APIs or quote and validate paths before running commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are directly visible and use path placeholders. Risk depends on the executing agent substituting raw user input.

content.user_title: Convert PDFs and Images to Markdown
content.value_statement: Teams often need readable text from scanned PDFs, screenshots, and image documents. This skill uses Claude vision OCR to extract content and save structured Markdown outputs.
actual_capabilities: Converts supported PDF files into one Markdown file or page-level Markdown files.
Converts PNG, JPG, JPEG, WEBP, GIF, BMP, and TIFF images into Markdown.
Uses Claude vision to preserve headings, tables, lists, code blocks, formulas, and diagrams.
Processes folders by splitting supported files into groups of three for parallel OCR work.
Accepts custom extraction instructions, such as table-only output or translated summaries.
Returns status summaries while keeping extracted document content out of the main context.
limitations: Very large files may fail with size limits before OCR completes.
OCR quality depends on image clarity, document layout, and readable source text.
The workflow writes Markdown files beside the original files and may need overwrite checks.
It does not use external OCR libraries such as PyPDF or Pillow.

use_cases.title: Archive scanned reports
use_cases.description: Convert scanned reports into Markdown so their text can be searched, reviewed, and summarized.

use_cases.title: Extract tables from PDFs
use_cases.description: Pull tabular information from PDF pages and save it as Markdown tables for review.

use_cases.title: Document screenshots
use_cases.description: Turn screenshots of interfaces, receipts, or notes into structured Markdown records.

prompt_templates.title: Convert one image
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this image to Markdown and preserve headings, lists, and visible labels: /path/to/image.png

prompt_templates.title: Convert one PDF
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this PDF to Markdown. Ask me whether to save one combined file or one file per page: /path/to/document.pdf

prompt_templates.title: Extract only tables
prompt_templates.prompt: OCR this PDF and extract only tables as Markdown tables. Mark unclear cells as unclear: /path/to/report.pdf

prompt_templates.title: Batch convert folder
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert all supported PDFs and images in this folder to Markdown. Keep each output beside its source file: /path/to/folder/

output_examples.input: A screenshot that contains a title, two bullet lists, and a small table.
output_examples.output: A Markdown file with a heading, two preserved lists, and a table with the visible rows and columns.

output_examples.input: A three-page PDF processed with page-level saving.
output: The skill reports that three page files were created.
Each output file contains the OCR text and structure for one page.
Unclear parts are marked instead of guessed.

output_examples.input: A folder with mixed PDF and image files.
output_examples.output: A status summary lists successful conversions, skipped files, and the output folder path.

best_practices: Review generated Markdown against the source document when accuracy matters.
Use focused custom instructions for tables, code, formulas, or translations.
Check for existing Markdown files before running batch conversion.
anti_patterns: Do not use it on documents that contain secrets unless the workspace is approved for that data.
Do not assume unclear text is accurate without checking the original file.
Do not run batch conversion on broad folders without confirming the file list first.

faq.question: What file types does this skill support?
faq.answer: It supports PDF, PNG, JPG, JPEG, WEBP, GIF, BMP, and TIFF files.

faq.question: Can it save one Markdown file per PDF page?
faq.answer: Yes. For PDFs, it can save one combined Markdown file or separate page-level files.

faq.question: Does it use external OCR libraries?
faq.answer: No. The workflow relies on Claude vision and explicitly avoids external OCR libraries.

faq.question: Can it process many files at once?
faq.answer: Yes. Folder mode groups supported files in batches of three for parallel processing.

faq.question: Will it preserve tables and code blocks?
faq.answer: It attempts to preserve tables, code blocks, headings, lists, formulas, and diagram descriptions.

faq.question: What should I check before publishing outputs?
faq.answer: Check unclear text, sensitive information, and whether any existing Markdown files could be overwritten.
